issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary variable (#aaaaaa) on --colour-bg-page (#ffffff) produces a contrast ratio of 2.85:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. This affects .section-header p, .hero-lead, .feature-card p, .step-content p, and other secondary text elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 43: --colour-text-secondary:   #595959;   /* WCAG AA on white */

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. An outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring when the outline width is zero. Keyboard users will not see any focus indicator, blocking navigation entirely.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 36px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 44×44 pixels. Small button variants still require adequate touch targets for accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none with only color change on hover. No underline, border, background highlight, or font-weight change provides non-colour distinction, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour.

issuesFound.title: No forced-colors/high-contrast-mode override for .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary button sets explicit background-color, border-color, and color values. The existing @media (forced-colors: active) block does not include overrides for button components, causing brand colours to potentially disappear in Windows High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static instead of position: absolute, with width: auto and height: auto instead of 1px dimensions. This makes screen-reader-only content visible on screen rather than hidden visually while remaining accessible to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) and transition: transform for its reveal animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 529 is empty and provides no override for this motion, affecting users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 44×44 pixels. Users with motor impairments or touch device users may have difficulty activating buttons reliably.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card:hover rule applies transform: translateY(-2px) for hover interaction. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for this selector, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus inset utility class lacks visible outline

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn rule includes “transition: … transform …” causing spatial movement, but there is no prefers‑reduced‑motion override to disable it for motion‑sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 480: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card:hover rule applies transform: translateY(-3px) for hover interaction. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for this selector, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card:hover rule applies transform: translateY(-2px) for hover interaction. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for this selector, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .stat-item:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item:hover rule applies transform: translateY(-2px) for hover interaction. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for this selector, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button::after pseudo-element uses transform: rotate(90deg) with transition for the accordion arrow animation. The existing override at line 1496 only sets transition: none but does not reset the transform value itself.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width/height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with no padding compensation. This is significantly below the 44×44 pixel minimum, making it difficult for touch users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation have text-decoration: none with only color change on hover. No underline, border, background highlight, or font-weight change provides non-colour distinction, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6
issuesFound.explanation: All heading elements use letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which resolves to -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing can reduce character recognition for users with dyslexia or low vision, impairing readability.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero h1 uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) which resolves to -0.05em. This aggressive negative spacing further reduces character recognition for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: No forced-colors/high-contrast-mode override for #nav-menu links and buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links and buttons set explicit color and background-color values. The existing @media (forced-colors: active) block does not include overrides for navigation elements, causing them to potentially become invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: No forced-colors/high-contrast-mode override for .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set explicit background-color and color values for both default and aria-pressed="true" states. The existing @media (forced-colors: active) block does not include overrides, causing state indicators to disappear in High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset and border-radius as replacements. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users with no visible indication of focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset. The base .btn class sets border: 2px solid transparent, which remains transparent on focus, resulting in no visible focus indicator for buttons.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible to sighted users and does not properly hide it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. It should use position: absolute with 1px dimensions and proper clipping.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size and falls in the 24-43px range without sufficient padding to compensate to 44px.

issuesFound.title: Global focus-visible removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none` and only an offset, providing no visible focus indicator for any interactive element. Keyboard‑only users lose the ability to see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: `:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Button focus-visible removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` rule removes the outline and does not supply any alternative visual cue, so focus on button elements is invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 727: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Button component lacks reduced‑motion override for transform
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` component includes a `transition` that animates `transform` (used on `:active` to move the button). No `@media (prefers‑reduced‑motion: reduce)` rule disables this motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 726: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with no compensating padding
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width of 28px and height of 28px (below the 44px recommended size) and padding is set to 0, providing no additional touch area. This creates a 28×28 px touch target that may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width of 20px and height of 20px, which is below the 24×24 px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.2 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this control.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on #search-input::placeholder
issuesFound.explanation: The placeholder text color #94a3b8 on background #f8fafc has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.7:1, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. This affects users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. This removes the focus ring for button elements specifically, overriding the global fix if it existed. No box-shadow or border is provided as a replacement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: The card body text uses #aaaaaa on white, failing contrast requirements (2.2:1). This affects readability of feature descriptions.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and width/height: auto, which makes content visible instead of hiding it visually. Screen-reader-only content should be visually hidden using absolute positioning and clipping.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 410 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the slide animation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). Padding does not compensate enough to reach 44px total height. This affects mobile and touch users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator replacement. Keyboard users navigating via Tab key will not see which button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: transform var(--transition-fast)), which causes spatial movement animations. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables or overrides this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector declares a transition that includes transform var(--transition-fast), causing spatial movement animations. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for this specific selector to disable the transform animation.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against var(--colour-neutral-900) (#0f172a) background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size. Small buttons are difficult to activate accurately on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px × 28px, which is below the 44px minimum. This icon button is too small for reliable touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is sized to 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum (and 44px recommended). This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to toggle.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px, which is below the 44px recommended size. While 24px is the absolute minimum, 44px is preferred for touch targets to ensure ease of use.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform and :active uses transform: translateY. There is no prefers-reduced-motion override for this component, causing motion for users who requested none.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies a transform scale/translate. The existing media query only targets .pricing-card:hover, so the featured card's specific animation persists for reduced-motion users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .hero-note
issuesFound.explanation: The text color #64748b on white background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.3:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum. This makes the social proof note hard to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph color #aaaaaa on white background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1, significantly below the 4.5:1 minimum. This text is very difficult to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .how-it-works > p
issuesFound.explanation: The section description text uses #aaaaaa on #f8fafc, failing contrast requirements. This impacts readability of the introductory text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .products-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The products section description uses #aaaaaa on white, failing contrast requirements. This text is too light for comfortable reading.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .filter-label
issuesFound.explanation: The filter label text #aaaaaa on #f1f5f9 background has very low contrast (~2.1:1). Users may not notice the label for the filter tabs.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .pricing-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing section description uses #aaaaaa on #f8fafc, failing contrast requirements. This reduces readability of the pricing intro.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: The input hint text #aaaaaa on white background fails contrast requirements (2.2:1). Helper text is critical for form completion and must be legible.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links use color #94a3b8 on dark background #0f172a. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.8:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social links use color #94a3b8 on dark background #0f172a, failing contrast requirements (3.8:1). These links may be invisible to users with contrast sensitivity.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom text uses #64748b on #0f172a. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.2:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum. Copyright and legal text becomes hard to read.

issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule removes the default focus outline (`outline: none`) and only adds an offset, leaving keyboard‑only users without any visible focus indicator. This blocks access for all interactive elements that rely on the default focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates its `transform` property on hover, creating spatial movement. No reduced‑motion override disables this animation for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 200: `.hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); }`  
    Line 202: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm (min-height: 20px)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card--featured:hover (translateY & scale)
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card animates with `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)` on hover, but no reduced‑motion override disables this for users preferring reduced motion.

Line 800: `.pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (translateY)
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button moves upward (`transform: translateY(-1px)`) on hover with a transition, but there is no reduced‑motion media query to suppress this motion, affecting users who request reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 260: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); }`  
    Line 262: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to hide content visually. It effectively does nothing, exposing screen-reader-only text to sighted users and cluttering the layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 373: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px × 20px. This is below the 24px minimum and significantly below the 44px recommended touch target size, making it hard to toggle on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1382: width: 24px; Line 1383: height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes a transform transition for interaction states. This motion is not disabled in the prefers-reduced-motion media query, potentially causing discomfort for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 753: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a 3D transform transition on hover. The prefers-reduced-motion override block at line 793 is empty and fails to set transform: none, meaning the animation persists for users who requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 794: .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: a
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor rule removes text-decoration without providing a non-colour alternative (like a border or weight change) in the base state. Users who cannot perceive colour differences may not identify these as links.

issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links explicitly remove underlines and rely on colour and background hover states. In the base state, colour alone distinguishes them, failing SC 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: [id$="-submenu"] li a

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible within forced-colors media query
issuesFound.explanation: Within the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator in high-contrast mode where system focus styles should be preserved or replaced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 365: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size without compensation on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no padding compensation, resulting in a touch target below the 44px recommended size. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate the links reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1104: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; padding: 10px 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 637: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size without compensation on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a touch target of only 28×28px. This is below the 44px recommended size and lacks compensating padding of at least 8px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 549: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 28px; height: 28px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 8px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets both min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult to hit at this size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 732: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Custom-styled checkboxes must meet the same minimum size requirements as other interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1091: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color changes (var(--colour-neutral-400) to var(--colour-neutral-0)) to indicate they are links. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color, as color alone is not sufficient to distinguish links from surrounding text for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1104: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus state removes the outline entirely (outline: none) and only sets outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 500: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible (forced-colors)
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible has outline: none without a visible replacement. This breaks focus visibility in high-contrast modes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 347: outline: 3px solid CanvasText;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 267: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the default outline but does not provide a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow. outline-offset alone is not visible. This prevents keyboard users from identifying focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 334: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at line 639 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 639: .skip-link { transform: none; transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes buttons hard to activate on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 417: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline but only adds an offset. No border or box-shadow is defined, making focus invisible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration (underlines). Body text links rely solely on colour to be identified, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 if colour perception is impaired.
issuesFound.fix: Line 259: text-decoration: underline;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The submenu link focus state removes the outline without a visible alternative, hindering keyboard navigation within dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 539: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search button focus state removes the outline without a visible border or box-shadow, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 583: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring entirely, making keyboard navigation invisible for users who rely on focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 355: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule overriding or disabling this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 624: (add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 447) .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties (transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower)) that creates spatial movement on hover. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 621 is empty and does not disable this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 621: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-slower); transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and can cause words to overlap at certain zoom levels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 179: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. Small checkboxes are difficult to toggle accurately on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the outline with “outline: none” and does not provide any visible replacement such as a box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline. Keyboard‑only users lose the focus indicator, making navigation impossible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 310: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets “word-spacing: -0.2em”, which compresses space between words and reduces readability, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 210: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule strips the outline for non‑keyboard focus without supplying an alternative visual cue, leaving focus invisible for mouse users who may rely on visual feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 317: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img rule animates “transform” (rotateY/rotateX) on load, but no prefers‑reduced‑motion media query disables or alters this animation, causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 860: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm variant sets “min-height: 20px”, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 44 × 44 px, making it difficult for finger navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card applies a combined “transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)” on hover, but there is no prefers‑reduced‑motion media query to suppress this animation, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1190: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links on a

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for input hints
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background. With a font-size of var(--font-size-xs) (12px), this small text has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector removes the outline entirely with outline: none and provides no replacement, leaving high-contrast mode users without any focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA) as there is no box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 215: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This negative spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to appear merged together at certain font sizes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 175: body { word-spacing: 0em; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset to -3px without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see focus on elements using this class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 232: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons include transform in their transition property, enabling spatial movement animations, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this for users who experience vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 615: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script) — Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 690: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: Featured pricing cards apply transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) on hover with a transition, but the reduced-motion media query only overrides the base .pricing-card:hover and does not include .pricing-card--featured:hover, leaving motion animations active for sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover, .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. This may create difficulty for users with motor impairments accessing submenu items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 490: [id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline with outline: none and provides only an outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 448: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set both min-width and min-height to 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. This creates a barrier for users who rely on touch input or have difficulty with precise pointer movements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 700: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); }

issuesFound.fix: Line 1030: .product-body .btn-secondary { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px, which meets the minimum but falls below the 44px recommended touch target size. The small vertical space may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1475: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: Hero images have a transition on transform properties creating 3D perspective animations, and the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 880 is empty, failing to disable these motion animations for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" link arrow has a transform transition that animates spatial movement (translateX) on hover, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this animation for users with motion sensitivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 840: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script) — Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a::after { transition: none; } .feature-card a:hover::after { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: Skip links use a transform transition to animate from off-screen to visible, which constitutes motion animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 305: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script) — Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input::placeholder and textarea::placeholder
issuesFound.explanation: Placeholder text uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.4:1. This fails the minimum 4.5:1 requirement for normal text under WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum), making placeholder content difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280: input::placeholder, textarea::placeholder { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); opacity: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .logo-bar-label
issuesFound.explanation: The logo bar label uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against background-color: var(--colour-neutral-50) (#f8fafc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.4:1. This fails the 4.5:1 minimum requirement, making the label difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 900: .logo-bar-label { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .hero-note
issuesFound.explanation: Hero note text uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.1:1. This fails the 4.5:1 minimum requirement for normal text under WCAG 1.4.3, making the social proof text difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 750: .hero-note { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .product-meta li
issuesFound.explanation: Product metadata uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.1:1. This fails the 4.5:1 minimum requirement, making product details difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1040: .product-meta li { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: Footer copyright text uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against a dark background (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.8:1. This fails the 4.5:1 minimum requirement, making legal text illegible for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1520: .footer-bottom > p { color: var(--colour-neutral-300); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-brand > p
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand description uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.1:1. This fails the 4.5:1 minimum requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1450: .footer-brand > p { color: var(--colour-neutral-300); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-brand address
issuesFound.explanation: Footer address text uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against a dark background (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.8:1. This fails the 4.5:1 minimum requirement, making contact information difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1455: .footer-brand address { color: var(--colour-neutral-300); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links inherit text-decoration: none from the global anchor reset and provide only a color change on hover without an underline, border-bottom, or background highlight. This relies solely on colour to distinguish links from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 555: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove underlines with text-decoration: none and provide only a colour change on hover (to white), relying solely on colour to identify links. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color as there is no non-colour visual differentiator such as underline or border-bottom.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1470: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove underlines with text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour changes (to white) on hover to indicate interactivity. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color as no non-colour visual differentiator is provided.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: .social-links a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .header-inner > a:first-child
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link inherits text-decoration: none and provides no hover underline, border-bottom, or background highlight to distinguish it as a link. This relies on colour alone, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415: .header-inner > a:first-child:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons suppress the focus outline without any replacement, preventing keyboard users from seeing the active filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1060: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the focus outline and do not provide a visible replacement, breaking focus visibility for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state applies a transform that rotates the image, with no prefers‑reduced‑motion override, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for motion‑sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 828: .hero-image img:hover { transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(0deg) rotateX(0deg); }
  Line 842: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a box-shadow or border replacement in the same block. Button focus is invisible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 496: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the table region is focused for scrolling.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which question is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1239: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard navigation in footer is invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1294: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.explanation: Text color #5588bb on transparent (inheriting #ffffff) has a contrast ratio of 3.73:1, failing WCAG AA for normal text.

issuesFound.explanation: Text color #5588bb on transparent (inheriting #ffffff) has a contrast ratio of 3.73:1, failing WCAG AA for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger text
issuesFound.explanation: Text color #ffffff on background #ee9999 has a contrast ratio of 2.07:1, failing WCAG AA (4.5:1) severely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 582: color: #991b1b;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .plan-recommended-badge text
issuesFound.explanation: Text color #ffffff on background #5588bb has a contrast ratio of 3.73:1, failing WCAG AA for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1205: color: #004aaa;

issuesFound.explanation: Text color #64748b on footer background #555555 has insufficient contrast (approx 1.5:1), failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. High contrast mode users rely on system focus indicators, and removing them entirely creates a critical accessibility barrier.
issuesFound.fix: Line 295: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus. The outline-offset property alone does not create a visible indicator when the outline width is zero.
issuesFound.fix: Line 285: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, meeting the minimum but falling below the recommended 44px touch target size without sufficient padding to compensate. This makes navigation difficult for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1246: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the default outline with outline: none and provides only an outline-offset. Without a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline value, keyboard users cannot see when the mobile navigation toggle has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 430: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Content intended only for screen readers will be visible to all users, breaking the visual design and potentially confusing sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 313: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has an explicit width and height of 28px without compensating padding to reach the recommended 44px touch target. While it meets the 24px minimum, the 28px size may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 506: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons must meet the minimum touch target size to be accessible for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links use color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against a background of var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.02:1, which fails the minimum 4.5:1 requirement for normal text and the 3:1 requirement for large text. Users with low vision cannot read these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1246: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating dropdown menus cannot see which link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 475: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. The button uses only outline: none and outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 503: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset without a box-shadow or border replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the "Learn more" links within feature cards have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: `.feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on filter tab buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector removes the default focus ring without providing a visible alternative. Users cannot see which filter button has keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on table wrapper
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. Keyboard users navigating the scrollable table region cannot see when the wrapper has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1082: `.table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without a visible alternative. Keyboard users expanding FAQ items cannot see which disclosure button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on form inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The selectors .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, and .form-group textarea:focus-visible remove the outline without providing a visible replacement in the same rule. While the :focus state has a box-shadow, the :focus-visible state explicitly sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0, overriding the focus style and leaving no indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: `.form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when checkbox inputs have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1224: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom links
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p a selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on a background of var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.02:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1258: `.footer-bottom > p a { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset without a box-shadow or border replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when social media links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1260: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer brand links
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when footer brand links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: `.footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 ratio 2.5:1
issuesFound.explanation: .footer-brand > p text color (#94a3b8) on footer background (#555555) has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.5:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1217: color: #ffffff;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements, making navigation impossible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 334: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces space between words, impairing readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 223: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset without a visible replacement (box-shadow or border). Keyboard users cannot identify which button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visually visible instead of hiding it. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 443: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height to 32px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 430: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration (underlines). Without underlines, links rely solely on colour to be distinguished from text, failing SC 1.4.1 for users with colour blindness.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 621: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input selector sets width and height to 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Custom-styled checkboxes must meet minimum size requirements or have sufficient padding to achieve a 24px minimum dimension.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1224: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class includes a transform transition property that causes spatial movement, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 367 is empty and does not disable this animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 367: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement on state changes. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 570: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes a transform transition that moves the button on hover/focus. No prefers-reduced-motion override exists to disable this spatial animation, which can cause issues for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 653: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes a transform transition that causes 3D rotation and spatial movement. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 696 is empty and does not disable this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 696: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img:hover selector applies a transform property that changes the perspective and rotation. Without a prefers-reduced-motion override, users with motion sensitivity may experience discomfort when hovering over the image.
issuesFound.fix: Line 696: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below the default. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap at certain zoom levels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 253: `body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against the footer background of var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555). The contrast ratio of approximately 2.02:1 is insufficient for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1262: `.social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 28px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom or font-weight change. Links are distinguished from surrounding text only by colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 248: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`
