issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput._onApply() missing aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the function displays an error message and announces it, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input field, so screen readers do not convey the invalid state programmatically.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2916: Inside the `else` block (when `result.valid` is false), add:
  setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module toggles the navigation drawer visible but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will not announce that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1767: Add the following line immediately after `_open = true;`:
  setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is collapsed, the close() function updates the DOM and emits an event but does not announce the collapsed state to screen readers via the liveRegion utility.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the _openInstance function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is now visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1927: Add the following line immediately after `inst.open = true;`:
  setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu changes visible content but does not announce this change to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the menu has appeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1932: Add the following line after the emit call:
  liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause toggle missing live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The play() and pause() functions update the button's aria-pressed state and label but do not announce the playing or paused state to screen readers via the liveRegion utility.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function for FAQ items expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3127: Add the following line immediately after `item.open = true;`:
  setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu removes it from view but fails to announce the collapsed state to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1956: Add the following line before the closing brace of the function:
  liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: When the email field fails the “required” or “email” rule, the validator only adds an error message element but never sets aria‑invalid="true", so AT does not know the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') when validation fails, and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu is opened the script adds CSS classes and focus‑traps but never updates aria‑expanded on .nav‑toggle to reflect the expanded state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') inside mobileNav.open after the menu is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open action does not announce the change via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown changes visible UI (menu items appear) but the code only emits a custom event; no aria‑live announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new options.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(…) with a concise description (e.g., “Main navigation menu opened”).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” function does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded the script adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the associated button, so the expanded state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening the item.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks aria‑pressed state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The button .nav‑toggle acts as a two‑state toggle (open/close) but never updates aria‑pressed, violating the requirement for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true') and 'false' respectively, or use toggleAttr to keep the state in sync.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons are not initialized with aria‑expanded when the markup omits it
issuesFound.explanation: If a FAQ item lacks data‑faq‑open or an explicit aria‑expanded attribute, the parser does not add aria‑expanded="false", leaving the button without a required state indicator.
issuesFound.fix: After creating trigger, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when no explicit state is present.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is now open, as the trigger state remains false or unset.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1355: function open(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation drawer but does not announce the "Navigation menu closed" state to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1370: function close(announceToSR) { ... if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); } ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The trigger's state remains inconsistent with the visible UI, confusing screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1525: function _openInstance(inst) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function opens the dropdown menu without announcing the change to screen readers. Users are not informed that a menu has opened, which is a critical status update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1525: function _openInstance(inst) { ... liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question button. The button state does not reflect the expanded panel, misleading screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2655: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function closes the dropdown menu without announcing the change to screen readers. Users are not informed that a menu has closed, which is a critical status update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1535: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... liveRegion.announce('Menu closed'); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved to the drawer content. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button and must manually navigate into the drawer, which may be missed if it appears off-screen.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1813: Add before the closing brace of the function:
  if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The _open() function marks the search state as open but relies on sub-functions to set aria-expanded. If the suggestion list is empty and no results are shown, aria-expanded may never be set to "true", leaving screen readers unaware the combobox is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2450: Add after `_searchState.open = true;`:
  if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “close” function does not set aria‑expanded to false on its trigger (redundant but required for symmetry)
issuesFound.explanation: While the close routine does call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') it does so **after** removing classes; however the earlier open function never set it to true, leaving the state inconsistent and potentially causing the close to set the attribute to false when it was never true. This can confuse AT that tracks state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure open sets aria‑expanded to true so that close correctly toggles it back to false.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle buttons lack aria‑pressed updates when the theme is changed via keyboard shortcut
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher updates data‑theme‑toggle buttons’ aria‑pressed only on click; when the theme is changed via the toggle() function (e.g., programmatically), the aria‑pressed state is not updated, leaving the button’s state out of sync for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In themeSwitcher.toggle or set ensure setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data‑theme‑toggle')===currentTheme)) so the attribute always reflects the current theme.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function collapses the answer panel but does not announce the "answer collapsed" state to screen readers. Users are not informed when an accordion item closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2685: function close(item, announceToSR) { ... if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60))); } ... }

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner function does not announce banner display to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._showBanner function displays the consent banner without announcing its appearance to screen readers. Users may be unaware that a dialog has appeared requiring interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3820: function _showBanner() { ... liveRegion.announce('Cookie consent banner displayed. Please review our cookie policy.'); ... }

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._hideBanner function does not announce banner dismissal to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._hideBanner function removes the consent banner without announcing its dismissal to screen readers. Users are not confirmed that their choice was registered and the banner is gone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3810: function _hideBanner() { ... liveRegion.announce('Cookie consent banner closed.'); ... }

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips._onChipClick function does not announce filter removal to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips._onChipClick function removes a filter chip but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed that a filter has been removed and results may have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2445: function _onChipClick(e) { ... liveRegion.announce(`Filter ${label} removed.`); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The helper function associates an error message with a field via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input element. This prevents screen readers from identifying the field as having a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~srUtils.markInvalid function: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the function after the guard clause `if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() omits aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the code fails to set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is expanded, violating the WAI-ARIA Disclosure pattern. The corresponding close() function correctly sets the attribute to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~mobileNav.open function: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown updates the DOM visibility but does not announce the state change to screen reader users via the liveRegion announcer, leaving users unaware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~dropdownMenu._openInstance: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` before the `emit` call. Line ~dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` before the `emit` call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() omits aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~dropdownMenu._openInstance function: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: While opening an FAQ item is announced to screen readers via liveRegion.announce(), closing an item is not announced. This inconsistency may leave users uncertain whether the collapse action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~faqAccordion.close function: Before the `emit` call, add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{title} — collapsed').replace('{title}', truncate(item.question, 60))); }`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons lack aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate active state only via CSS classes. They require aria-pressed (or aria-selected if using tab roles) to communicate the active filter state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function: Inside the forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() omits aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ item updates the visual state and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~faqAccordion.open function: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function in inlineValidator adds CSS error classes but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on fields that fail validation, nor removes it on success. Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey error state.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when result.valid is false call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and when true remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not set aria‑expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The function _openInstance in the dropdown menu controller adds the “open” CSS class and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but it never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (selector .nav‑toggle) toggles the mobile drawer via mobileNav.toggle, but the script never updates aria‑pressed to reflect the button’s pressed state. Users of assistive tech cannot tell whether the menu is currently open.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.toggle (or mobileNav.open/close) add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open)); so the button’s state is announced.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not set aria‑expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in the FAQ accordion sets aria‑expanded="false" when closing, but when a question is opened it never updates aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button. This leaves the expanded state invisible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After item.open is set to true, call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); before animating the panel.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed the script only emits custom events ( dropdown:open / dropdown:close ) and updates ARIA attributes, but it never writes a message to a live‑region. Users relying on screen readers receive no audible feedback that the menu has changed state.
issuesFound.fix: After _openInstance and _closeInstance add liveRegion.announce( item.open ? 'Menu opened' : 'Menu closed' ); or update a dedicated role="status" element.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play/pause button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel’s play/pause control (data‑carousel‑play) toggles automatic rotation via togglePlay, but the button never updates aria‑pressed to indicate whether autoplay is active. This violates the toggle‑button pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In togglePlay (and the play / pause functions) call setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing)); after changing the state.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips removal not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a user clicks a chip’s “×” button, activeFilterChips._onChipClick removes the chip and updates the filter state, but no live‑region message is emitted. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the filter was cleared.
issuesFound.fix: After render() or within _onChipClick, call liveRegion.announce( 'Filter removed' ); or a more specific message indicating which filter was cleared.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an aria-describedby reference but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the field's invalid state, even though the error message is linked. The corresponding markValid function attempts to remove aria-invalid, creating an asymmetry that leaves fields permanently marked as valid in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class and locks scroll but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now expanded, as the trigger retains its initial false state. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the menu and resets the trigger state but does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed that the navigation menu has closed, whereas the open function does announce "Navigation menu opened".

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves the trigger in a false state despite the menu being visible. The close counterpart correctly sets aria-expanded="false", causing inconsistency in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function displays dynamic content (the menu panel) in response to user interaction but does not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified that the menu has opened, which is a status message requirement for dynamic content updates.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel pause function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.pause function stops the auto-rotation of slides but does not announce this mode change to screen readers. Users are not informed that the carousel has paused, leaving them unaware that slides will no longer advance automatically.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close instance lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function hides the menu panel but does not announce this state change to screen readers. While the aria-expanded attribute is updated, the lack of a live region announcement means users are not explicitly informed that the menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function fails to update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. This prevents screen readers from conveying that the answer is now visible. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", leading to a state desynchronization.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons use invalid aria-selected attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons are native `<button>` elements that use aria-selected, which is not a valid attribute for buttons. Buttons should use aria-pressed to indicate a pressed state, or the elements must have role="tab" to validly use aria-selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~pricing._syncToggleState function: Change `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` to `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer behaves as a modal dialog (traps focus, disables background interaction) but lacks aria-modal="true" on the drawer element. This attribute is required to inform assistive technologies that the content underneath is inert.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~mobileNav.init function: After the line `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);`, add `if (_drawer) _drawer.setAttribute('aria-modal', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: When the carousel initializes with autoplay disabled, the play/pause button does not receive an initial aria-pressed attribute, leaving its toggle state ambiguous to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~testimonialsCarousel.init function: After `_initialised = true;`, add `if (_playBtn) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String((CONFIG.carousel ? CONFIG.carousel.autoPlay !== false : true) && !prefersReducedMotion()));`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form binds fields with inlineValidator.bind but the validator (see Issue 6) does not apply aria‑invalid. Consequently, when the email field is invalid the error is not exposed to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the newsletter form’s email input receives aria‑invalid="true" via the inline validator’s _renderResult logic.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close removes CSS classes and adds hidden to the drawer, but it never sets aria‑hidden="true". Some browsers keep the element in the accessibility tree when only hidden is used, leaving the off‑canvas menu reachable by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function collapses the answer panel but does not announce this change to screen readers. The open function announces "answer expanded", but the close function fails to announce "answer collapsed", leaving users unaware of the state change.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent accept handler lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onAccept function processes the user's consent choice and hides the banner but does not announce the successful save to screen readers. Users are not confirmed that their preferences have been recorded, which is a critical feedback loop for form-like interactions.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent decline handler lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onDecline function processes the user's refusal of non-essential cookies but does not announce the action to screen readers. Similar to the accept handler, this lacks confirmation feedback for the user's decision.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.play function starts the auto-rotation of slides but does not announce this mode change to screen readers. Users are not informed that slides will now advance automatically, which affects their ability to consume content at their own pace.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() omits aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Consequently, screen readers do not announce the invalid state of form fields to users during validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1035: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function, before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to show the mobile navigation drawer and announces the state change via liveRegion, but never sets the aria-expanded attribute to "true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1126: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: While the module initializes a focus trap (_trap) in the init() function, the open() function never calls _trap.activate(). Keyboard focus is not confined to the mobile menu when opened, allowing users to tab behind the overlay to background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1126: Add `_trap.activate();` after the `_lockScroll();` call (or before the emit call).

issuesFound.title: Pricing price updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods, the price values update visually (and animate), but the new price amounts are not announced to screen readers. Users hear "Billing period changed to annual" but are not informed of the new price values unless they navigate to the price elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3220: After updating plan.priceDisplay.textContent, add an announcement of the new price: `liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target} for ${plan.id} plan`);` (or similar appropriate message).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but does not update the trigger's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The corresponding _closeInstance() sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1318: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the function body.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: When opening the search suggestions panel, the function sets internal state and shows recent queries, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the search input element. The _close() function sets it to "false", so screen readers only hear the collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2418: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria‑invalid (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator module’s _validate() function marks fields as invalid visually but does not set aria‑invalid="true" on the input element, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult(), when result.valid is false, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and remove it when valid.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module’s open() function toggles the drawer’s visibility but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module’s _debouncedFetch() updates the suggestion list UI but never writes to an aria‑live region to announce the number of results, leaving users unaware of changes.
issuesFound.fix: After suggestionListRenderer.render() call liveRegion.announce(`${count} suggestions available`); or similar.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module registers dropdown triggers but does not set aria‑expanded="false" on the trigger buttons during initialization, causing an undefined state for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In register() or during init, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); for each trigger.

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module’s setPeriod() changes the displayed price but does not announce the change to screen readers, so users are not informed that the billing period switched.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call liveRegion.announce(`Billing period changed to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion module’s toggle() function expands/collapses FAQ items by adding/removing CSS classes but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In open() and close() add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true' or 'false');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the panel to visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers announce the item as collapsed even when it is visually expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1670: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function updates the DOM and focus trap but does not call liveRegion.announce to inform screen reader users that the navigation menu has closed. The open function announces correctly, creating an inconsistent experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1305: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function collapses the answer panel but does not call liveRegion.announce. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has collapsed, unlike the open action which is announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1697: liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to "false", causing screen readers to incorrectly report the menu state as closed when it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1275: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu instance functions do not announce state changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle visibility but do not write to a live region. Users relying on screen readers are not notified when dropdown menus open or close.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1425: liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');
  Line 1442: liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to "false", leading to incorrect accessibility tree state for dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1425: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed attribute (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module’s setPeriod() function updates the visual state of the period buttons but does not set aria‑pressed on the period buttons to reflect the active state, violating ARIA button state guidelines.
issuesFound.fix: In _setSyncToggleState() or within _setSyncToggleState(), add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); for each period button.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑controls (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module’s register() adds aria‑haspopup but does not set aria‑controls to reference the associated panel’s id, breaking the relationship between trigger and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the panel has an id (e.g., srUtils.ensureId(panel) ) and set setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panel.id);

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The navKeyboardShortcuts module registers shortcut keys but the associated navigation buttons (e.g., “Open navigation menu”) are not given aria‑pressed to indicate their toggled state.
issuesFound.fix: When toggling the navigation (e.g., mobileNav.toggle()), also set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(isOpen));

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not cleared on valid input (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator module clears visual error styles but does not remove aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid, causing the error state to persist for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult() when result.valid is true, call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent accept and decline handlers do not announce result to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onAccept and _onDecline functions hide the banner and update analytics but do not announce the consent decision to screen readers. Users are not confirmed that their choice was registered.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2685: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved.');
  Line 2694: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved.');

issuesFound.title: Focus trap activation fails due to missing SUPPORTS.dom property
issuesFound.explanation: The createFocusTrap.activate function checks SUPPORTS.dom before attaching event listeners, but the SUPPORTS object does not define a dom property. This prevents focus trap listeners from ever being attached, allowing focus to escape modals and drawers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 935: if (true) { // SUPPORTS.dom check removed as property is missing

issuesFound.title: Search and FAQ highlighting fails due to missing REGEX.escapeRegex
issuesFound.explanation: searchResultsHighlighter and faqAccordion attempt to use REGEX.escapeRegex to sanitize search terms, but this property is not defined in the REGEX object. This causes TypeError exceptions when users search, breaking the highlight functionality.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2047: const escaped = token.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
  Line 1763: const escaped = token.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation body class toggle fails due to CSS property mismatch
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav references CSS.navOpen to toggle the body class, but the CSS object defines NAV_OPEN. The class is never applied or removed, potentially breaking scroll lock and visual styles associated with the open state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1277: addClass(document.body, CSS.NAV_OPEN);
  Line 1307: removeClass(document.body, CSS.NAV_OPEN);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu outside click detection fails due to missing SELECTORS.primaryNav
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu references SELECTORS.primaryNav to detect outside clicks/focus, but this selector is not defined in the SELECTORS object (mainNav exists instead). The dropdown may fail to close when focus moves outside the navigation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1498: const nav = qs(SELECTORS.mainNav);
  Line 1507: const nav = qs(SELECTORS.mainNav);

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation utility links error messages via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clears it on valid fields. Screen readers cannot identify which fields have failed validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050 (in markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before the closing brace. Line 2060 (in markValid): After removing aria-describedby, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panel visibility is toggled, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is not synchronized. This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern and leaves assistive technology users unaware of the panel state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2630 (in _openInstance): After `inst.open = true;`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line 2650 (in _closeInstance): After `inst.open = false;`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible UI but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the live region system.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2420 (in open()): After opening, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`. Line 2440 (in close()): Before closing, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true" (the close() function only sets it to "false"). Screen readers cannot convey the expanded state of the panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120 (in open()): After `item.open = true;`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus open and close without announcing their state changes, leaving screen reader users unaware that a panel has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2630 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`. Line 2650 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates price values visually but does not announce the new period or prices to screen readers, making the dynamic content change invisible to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3360 (in setPeriod(), after _syncToggleState): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Switched to annual billing' : STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Switched to monthly billing');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" during setup, leaving screen readers unaware of the initial collapsed state of the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480 (in init(), after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`): Add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" but is not initialized with aria-expanded="false", violating the ARIA combobox pattern for a collapsed suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3450 (in mount(), after setting aria-haspopup): Add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the code adds error‑message CSS classes but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the input, so AT cannot convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the !result.valid branch add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); (and ensure the attribute is removed on success).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened, the code sets CSS classes and animates the panel but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the trigger button, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening and setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close actions are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown controller emits custom events but does not write to an ARIA live region after a panel is opened or closed, leaving users of assistive technology unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling the panel, update a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> with a message such as “Menu opened” or “Menu closed”.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not set aria‑expanded on the trigger (duplicate of Issue 1 but for additional triggers)
FAQ accordion state changes are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a FAQ item does not send an announcement to a live region, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback about the change.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the state, call liveRegion.announce(`Answer for ${item.question} expanded`); or similar, and a corresponding message for collapse.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle (annual/monthly) does not announce change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices but does not send an announcement, so AT users cannot know which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call liveRegion.announce(`Billing period set to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);.

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle button does not announce mode change
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the grid/list view button changes layout classes but provides no live‑region feedback, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new view mode.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call liveRegion.announce(`Switched to ${mode} view`);.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` utility function adds an error description via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on `aria-invalid` to announce that a field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1975: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's open/closed state changes dynamically.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2345: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 2370: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field

issuesFound.fix: Line 1995: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); if (isElement(errorEl) && errorEl.id) { ...

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in the `dropdownMenu` module show and hide dropdown panels but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern for keyboard and screen reader users.

  Line 2595: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce count changes to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function updates the product count badge text and `aria-label` but does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users filtering products will not know the result count has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3845: _updateCountBadge(totalMatches); liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} shown`);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce price changes to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function updates all pricing card values when the billing period toggles but does not announce the price change to screen readers. Users switching between monthly and annual billing will not be notified of the update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4145: emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period }); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices updated');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `faqAccordion` module expand and collapse answer panels but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the question trigger (`item.trigger`). Assistive technologies will not reflect the current state of the accordion items.
issuesFound.fix:

  Line 4375: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing FAQ panels changes the visible content but does not announce the state change or the question title to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120 (in open()): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title) : `${item.title} expanded`);`. Line 3140 (in close()): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title) : `${item.title} collapsed`);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Applying filters updates the product grid and count badge but does not announce the number of results or that filtering has occurred, leaving screen reader users unaware the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2830 (after _updateCountBadge): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens without trapping focus, allowing keyboard users to tab outside the drawer into the underlying page while the drawer remains open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2420 (in open(), after _lockScroll()): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button lacks an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element, preventing screen readers from identifying the relationship between the button and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480 (in init(), after selecting _drawer): Add `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing role
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons use aria-selected to indicate state but lack role="tab", making the tab pattern semantically invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3390 (in init(), inside the _periodBtns.forEach or after selecting them): Add `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => setAttr(btn, 'role', 'tab'));` and ensure the container has `setAttr(periodContainer, 'role', 'tablist');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: On successful validation the code removes error classes but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the field permanently marked as invalid for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In the result.valid branch add field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); or setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation does not set aria‑invalid on the email field
issuesFound.fix: After const result = validateField(emailVal, ['required','email']); if !result.valid call setAttr(_emailEl,'aria-invalid','true'); else removeAttribute.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are hidden with hidden attribute but not aria‑hidden, violating the requirement that hidden content be removed from the accessibility tree when not using hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(item.panel,'aria-hidden','true'); when closing and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function adds an error element to the field's aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2964 (in markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in dropdownMenu show and hide the dropdown panels but fail to set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1744 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line 1758 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of the function before `if (!inst.open) return;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function dynamically shows/hides product cards and updates the count badge, but it does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen readers via a live region. Users are not notified that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3114 (after _updateCountBadge call): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the FAQ answer panel and sets item.open = true, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetric state where screen readers cannot detect when an item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3424 (in open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates visible prices and billing period labels when users toggle between monthly/annual billing, but it does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3704 (after _syncToggleState call): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs lack Enter/Space keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs use createRovingTabindex for arrow navigation, but if the tab elements (matching `[data-filter-value]`) are not native buttons, they lack Enter/Space activation. The _onTabClick handler only responds to mouse clicks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3240 (in productFilter.init, after creating roving tabindex): Ensure tabs have `role="button"` and add keydown handler for Enter/Space that calls _onTabClick, or change selector to use native button elements.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons lack Enter/Space keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (matching `[data-period]`) use createRovingTabindex for arrow navigation, but if not native buttons, they lack Enter/Space activation. The _onPeriodBtnClick handler is only bound to click.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3820 (in pricing.init): Ensure period buttons have `role="button"` and add keydown handler for Enter/Space, or use native button elements.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module initializes the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the search input element during initialization. The attribute is only set dynamically when opening/closing the list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2534 (in mount function, after wiring ARIA): Add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field missing validation and aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription form does not bind any validation to the email `<input>`. Consequently, invalid emails are not marked with `aria-invalid="true"`, breaking the required ARIA mapping for form errors.
issuesFound.fix: In `newsletterForm.init`, after locating `_emailEl`, call `inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required', 'email'])`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_openInstance` (and its counterpart `_closeInstance`) in the dropdown menu controller toggles the visibility of the panel but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search status updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The helper `_updateStatus` changes the text of `_statusEl` to reflect search results, but `_statusEl` lacks an `aria-live` attribute, so screen‑reader users do not hear the status change.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite')` (or `assertive` for errors) when creating `_statusEl`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑expanded on init
issuesFound.explanation: In the `register` function of the dropdown menu, the trigger button is given `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` but lacks an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `aria-controls`, also call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown via `_openInstance` / `_closeInstance` emits custom events but does not provide a screen‑reader announcement, leaving users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After emitting the event, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')` with appropriate text.

issuesFound.title: Period toggle buttons lack aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing period buttons (`data-period`) are updated with `aria-selected` but never set `aria-pressed`, which is required for a toggle button to convey its pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState`, replace `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', ...)` with `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', ...)` (or add both).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce new pricing
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period via `pricing.setPeriod` updates prices visually but does not announce the new pricing to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of `setPeriod`, after updating all plan cards, call `liveRegion.announce('Pricing updated to ' + BILLING_PERIODS[period].label + ' billing')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqRegistry.parse`, each FAQ trigger receives `aria-controls` but never receives an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, leaving the collapsed state ambiguous to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `aria-controls`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel open/close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the FAQ accordion emit custom events but do not announce the change via a live region, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive which answer is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: In `open`, after expanding, call `liveRegion.announce('Answer expanded: ' + item.question)`; similarly, in `close`, announce the collapse.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the code only removes CSS classes; it never sets `aria-hidden="true"` or the `hidden` attribute, so the drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.close`, after removing the open class, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` (and remove it on open).

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid()` function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Assistive technologies rely on `aria-invalid` to identify the field as erroneous.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1547: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded state sync
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions toggle the visual state of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1697: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1727: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close action missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function hides the navigation drawer but does not announce this state change to screen readers via a live region. Users may not realize the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1727: if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init()` function does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute on the `_trigger` element. Assistive technologies may not correctly identify the button's initial state as collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1787: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` and `faqAccordion.close()` functions change the visible content (expanding/collapsing answers) but do not announce these changes to screen readers. Users may not know which answer is currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2697: if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));
  Line 2727: if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded state sync
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()` functions manage the panel visibility but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `inst.trigger` button. The toggle state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1897: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1917: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger missing aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function sets the item state to open but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `item.trigger`. The `close()` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2697: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter grid changes missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters()` function updates the visible product grid and count badge but does not announce the new result count or state change to screen readers. Users are not informed that the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2297: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden on close
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function removes the visual open class but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the `_drawer` element. The navigation content may remain in the accessibility tree even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1727: if (_drawer) _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" (or remove the attribute) to indicate the field is no longer invalid. Screen readers may continue to report the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2976 (in markValid): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after the aria-describedby cleanup logic.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion triggers lack Enter/Space keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers are assigned role="button" and tabindex="0" when not native buttons, and they handle click events for toggling. However, the _onKeydown handler only manages Arrow Up/Down/Home/End navigation and does not handle Enter or Space keys to activate the toggle. Keyboard users cannot expand/collapse items unless they are native buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3454 (in _onKeydown, before default case): Add cases for `case KEYS.ENTER:` and `case KEYS.SPACE:` that call `e.preventDefault(); toggle(item);`

issuesFound.title: planComparisonHighlighter headers lack keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: The planComparisonHighlighter delegates click events to `[data-plan-head]` elements (likely table headers) to scroll to plan cards, but these are not focusable or activatable via keyboard. There is no tabindex, role, or keydown handler for Enter/Space.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3944 (in init, after delegate): Add `setAttr(el, 'tabindex', '0');` and `setAttr(el, 'role', 'button');` to plan headers, and add keydown handler for Enter/Space that triggers the same scroll action.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation hamburger button (`_trigger`) never receives an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern for menus.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init`, after locating `_trigger`, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in product filter lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters` updates the product count badge and empty‑state message, but does not announce these changes to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`)`; after showing the empty state, announce “No products match your filters”.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod()` function updates all visible prices on the page when the billing period changes but does not announce this significant content update to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2497: liveRegion.announce(period === 'annual' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `scrollToTop._onClick()` function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce this action to screen readers. Users may be disoriented by the sudden viewport change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2097: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide the dropdown panels but do not synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether a dropdown menu is currently open or closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function discovers the navigation toggle button but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen readers may announce an undefined or incorrect initial state for the menu button.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2860: Insert after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`:


issuesFound.title: filterTabs missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() function updates the active CSS class on filter tab buttons but does not set the aria-pressed attribute to indicate the pressed state. Screen readers cannot convey which filter tab is currently active.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3670: Insert inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`:
