
issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init() function sets up the search input as a combobox but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen readers cannot correctly announce the initial collapsed state of the suggestion dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3280: Insert after `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`:
  ```javascript
      setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);
  ```

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function closes the navigation drawer and removes it from view, but unlike the open() function, it does not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2820: Insert after `if (!_open) return;`:
  ```javascript
      liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');
  ```

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input does not reflect validation error via aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: In `newsletterForm._onSubmit()` the email field is validated with `validateField`, but when the validation fails the code only displays a status message and never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the `<input>` element. Screen‑reader users receive no indication of the error.
issuesFound.fix: When `!result.valid`, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on successful validation or after correcting the error, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function changes the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but never updates the `aria-expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). This leaves screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!_open));` in both `open()` and `close()` (or within `toggle()`).

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle buttons lack live‑region announcement of mode change
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._onViewToggle()` function switches between grid and list view and updates ARIA attributes, but it does not announce the change to screen‑reader users. Users may not realize the layout has changed.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the view mode, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.viewModeChanged || 'View mode switched to ' + mode);`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing initial aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqRegistry.parse()` function creates the FAQ trigger button but does not set an initial `aria-expanded` value (defaults to undefined). The button’s expanded state is only set when the user interacts, leaving the initial state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the trigger, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(isInitiallyOpen));`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not announce period change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod()` function updates the displayed prices when the billing period changes, but it does not provide an audible cue. Users relying on AT may not notice the price update.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period set to ' + period);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without aria-hidden, breaking accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element (`_drawer`). Although the drawer is visually hidden, it remains perceivable to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()`, after hiding the drawer, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()`, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function marks a field as invalid by updating aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field element. This is a critical failure for form validation accessibility (WCAG 3.3.1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2865: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's state change. Additionally, mobileNav.init() does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function clears the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute from the field element. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2878: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and dropdownMenu._closeInstance() functions manage the panel visibility but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technologies from detecting the open/closed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1488: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1504: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() and productFilter._updateCountBadge() functions update the visible product count badge (_countBadge) but do not announce this change to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the number of results changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1942: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons use aria-selected instead of aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState() function sets aria-selected on button elements ([data-period]) that act as toggle controls. Buttons should use aria-pressed to indicate their state, whereas aria-selected is reserved for tabs or listbox options.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2155: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). While faqAccordion.close() correctly sets it to false, the open state is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2335: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator number change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator._animate() function dynamically updates the textContent of statistic elements (e.g., "10,000+ clients") without using an aria-live region. Screen reader users will not hear the numbers being counted up.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3365: el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader hidden state not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The stickyHeader._onScroll() function adds the CSS class 'header--hidden' to hide the header visually (likely via transform or position) but does not set aria-hidden="true". The header remains in the accessibility tree and may be focusable while invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1695: setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');
  Line 1702: setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-controls on the trigger button to reference the navigation drawer element. Screen readers cannot establish the relationship between the toggle button and the content it controls.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2860: Insert after `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);`:
  ```javascript
      if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));
  ```

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates price amounts and billing period labels dynamically, but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when prices switch between monthly and annual billing.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3880: Insert after `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`:
  ```javascript
      liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);
  ```

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick() function scrolls the page to the top when activated, but does not announce this navigation action via a live region. Screen reader users may be disoriented by the sudden scroll without confirmation of what occurred.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3120: Insert after `window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' });`:
  ```javascript
      liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');
  ```

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function reveals the navigation drawer but does not move keyboard focus into the drawer. Keyboard users remain focused on the trigger button and must manually navigate to the opened menu, creating a barrier to access.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the navigation drawer but does not return focus to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users are left with a lost focus context, potentially at the bottom of the page or in an undefined location.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2820: Insert before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`:
  ```javascript
      if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();
  ```

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the answer panel but does not move focus into the revealed content. Keyboard users must manually navigate into the newly visible content to read it.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close() function collapses the answer panel but does not return focus to the trigger button. Keyboard users may lose their place in the page when the content disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4230: Insert at the end of the close() function:
  ```javascript
      item.trigger.focus();
  ```

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels hidden only with `hidden` attribute, missing explicit aria-hidden when toggled via script
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.close()` function sets `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but does not also set `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel. While the `hidden` attribute removes the element from the accessibility tree, some AT implementations rely on `aria-hidden` for dynamic changes.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `hidden`, also call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; ensure `open()` removes the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu items hidden via CSS class without aria-hidden when off‑screen
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance()` function hides the dropdown panel by removing a CSS class and later adding `hidden` after a transition, but during the transition the panel remains in the accessibility tree without `aria-hidden`. This can cause brief exposure of hidden content to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Immediately before starting the hide transition, set `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; remove the attribute when showing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer focus trap not released on close, potentially trapping keyboard focus
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function deactivates the focus trap only if it was active, but does not ensure that focus returns to the trigger button. If the drawer is closed via external click, focus may remain inside the hidden drawer.
issuesFound.fix: After closing, call `_trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });` to return focus to the hamburger button.

issuesFound.title: toastManager dismiss does not set aria-hidden during animation
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager.dismiss() function adds a CSS class to hide the toast visually but does not set aria-hidden="true" immediately. During the hide animation, the toast remains in the accessibility tree, potentially confusing screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: setAttr(toastEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');

issuesFound.title: viewportReveal elements not managed for accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The viewportReveal._reveal() function makes elements visible by adding a CSS class but does not manage aria-hidden. Elements that are initially hidden (e.g., opacity: 0) should have aria-hidden="true" until revealed to prevent them from being perceived as empty or broken.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3495: el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 3510: el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); /* In init for unrevealed */

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick() function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce this action to screen readers via a live region. Users may lose context of their new position on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1785: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips render not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips.render() function updates the list of filter chips dynamically but does not announce the change to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filters are added or removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2075: liveRegion.announce('Filters updated');

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm validation relies on broken utility
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletterForm._onSubmit() function calls inlineValidator.validate(), which relies on srUtils.markInvalid(). Since srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid (see Issue 4), this form's validation errors are not properly exposed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2865: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); /* Fix in srUtils */

issuesFound.title: contactForm validation relies on broken utility
issuesFound.explanation: The contactForm._onSubmit() function calls inlineValidator.validate(), which relies on srUtils.markInvalid(). Since srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid (see Issue 4), this form's validation errors are not properly exposed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2865: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); /* Fix in srUtils */

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function adds aria-describedby to link fields with error messages but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Conversely, markValid() does not remove aria-invalid or set it to "false". Assistive technologies cannot identify which fields have validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4724 (markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`. Line 4733 (markValid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` after clearing the describedby.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide dropdown panels but fail to toggle the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3024 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line 3039 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the start of the function before the setTimeout.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product cards and the count badge, but it does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of applyFilters (inside the filterAnimation.run callback), add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open() and _close() functions manage the visibility of the suggestion listbox but do not toggle aria-expanded on the combobox input (_inputEl). Since the input has role="combobox", it must communicate its expanded state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3574 (in _open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line 3589 (in _close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates price displays across all plan cards when switching billing periods, but it does not announce the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod, after updating the toggle UI, add: `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function toggles between monthly and annual billing periods via buttons, but it does not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button or aria-pressed="false" on the inactive button. Screen readers cannot identify which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: In the setPeriod function, after determining the new period, add: `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period; setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); });`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.play() and pause() functions toggle auto-play state but do not update aria-pressed on the play/pause button (_playBtn). The button acts as a toggle but lacks the pressed state attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4438 (in play): Add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` after `_playing = true;`. Line 4448 (in pause): Add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` after `_playing = false;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" when the dropdown is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: In register(), after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not announce the state change ("Navigation opened" / "Navigation closed") via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2844 (open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`. Line 2864 (close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not establish an aria-controls relationship between the navigation toggle button and the navigation drawer (main-nav), even though the drawer has an ID (#main-nav per SELECTORS). Users cannot identify which element the button controls.
issuesFound.fix: In init(), after setting _drawer and _trigger, add: `if (_trigger && _drawer && !_trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || 'main-nav');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init() function creates a combobox input with role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox" but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" when the suggestion panel is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: In init(), after mounting the listbox and setting up the input, add: `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate` routine marks fields with visual error classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid inputs, so AT does not announce errors.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate` after a failed validation, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `openInstance` function in the dropdown menu controller toggles the visibility of the panel but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` emit custom events but do not announce the state change to a screen‑reader via an ARIA live region, violating the requirement to inform users of navigation drawer state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened')`; after closing, announce the closed state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.init` the trigger buttons are given `aria‑haspopup` but no initial `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute, so the accessibility tree does not reflect the closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: After creating each trigger, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panel open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` and `close` functions change visibility but do not provide an ARIA live announcement of the expanded/collapsed state, so users are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' – answer expanded')`; after closing, announce the collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (`data-period`) are toggled with CSS classes only; they never receive `aria‑pressed` updates, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState` set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))` for each period button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle (`data-billing-toggle`) changes visual state but never updates `aria‑pressed`, leaving the state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick` after changing period, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'))`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ question button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands an answer panel but never sets `aria‑expanded` on the associated trigger button, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item, …)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close` set it to `"false"`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod` updates the UI but does not provide an ARIA live announcement of the new billing period, leaving AT users uninformed.
issuesFound.fix: After setting the period, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged?.replace('{period}', period) || 'Billing period switched to ' + period)`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3353: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open and close functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1376: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1396: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters count change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter applyFilters function updates the product count badge text and aria-label but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2673: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init function does not set the initial aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. The state is undefined until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1425: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod price change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing setPeriod function updates price displays across all plan cards but does not announce the billing period change or price update via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the context change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2343: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle the visibility of the panel but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button (inst.trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1593: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1606: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator animate text change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator _animate function updates the text content of stat elements dynamically but does not announce the final value via a live region. Screen reader users may miss the updated statistic.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3838: liveRegion.announce(`Statistic updated to ${_format(cfg.target, cfg)}`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialize aria-expanded on the trigger. The initial state is missing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1675: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: suggestionListRenderer mount does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer mount function sets aria-haspopup and role="combobox" on the input but does not initialize aria-expanded. The initial state is undefined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1775: setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._onViewToggle() function toggles between grid and list view modes but does not set aria-pressed on the view mode buttons to indicate which view is active.
issuesFound.fix: In _onViewToggle, after determining the mode, add: `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { const isPressed = b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode; setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', String(isPressed)); });`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions toggle dropdown visibility but do not announce the expanded/collapsed state to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3024 (_openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(\`\${inst.trigger.textContent} expanded\`);`. Line 3039 (_closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(\`\${inst.trigger.textContent} collapsed\`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never instantiates it with createFocusTrap(). When the mobile menu opens, focus is not trapped within the navigation drawer, allowing users to tab outside the modal context.
issuesFound.fix: In the open() function, after `_open = true;`, add: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true }); _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function deactivates the focus trap (if it existed) but does not explicitly return keyboard focus to the trigger button that opened the menu. Focus is lost when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of close(), before the emit call, add: `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the validator removes visual error styling but does not remove `aria-invalid`, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In the success branch of `inlineValidator.validate`, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `_closeInstance` toggle visibility but do not send any live‑region updates, so AT users cannot perceive menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened?.replace('{label}', trigger.textContent.trim()) || 'Menu opened')`; after closing, announce closure.

issuesFound.title: Search result count updates are not announced via a dedicated live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateStatus` updates a status element that has `aria-live="polite"` but the element is reused for multiple messages (error, no results, count). When the result count changes, the same element may already contain the same text, causing some AT to suppress the announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the status element’s text is always changed (e.g., prepend a zero‑width space) or use a separate live region for count updates.

issuesFound.title: Toggle functions that hide elements via CSS class removal do not set aria‑hidden when the hidden technique is not `display:none`
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close` removes the `is-open` class from the drawer but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element, and the drawer is hidden only by CSS (no `display:none`). AT may still expose the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: In `close`, after removing the class, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open`, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Toggle widgets that hide content via off‑screen positioning lack aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation overlay (`_overlay`) is hidden by adding/removing the `hidden` attribute, but the drawer itself is only moved off‑screen via CSS when closed, without `aria-hidden`.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer receives `aria-hidden="true"` when closed and the attribute is removed when opened.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open function expands the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state management.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3493: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module's _open and _close functions show/hide the suggestion list but do not update aria-expanded on the search input (_inputEl). Screen reader users are not informed of the list visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1933: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1943: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils markValid function removes the error description but fails to set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field may remain in an invalid state programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3366: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.fix:
Line 1754: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);
  Line 1769: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the visible product count updates and the grid refreshes, but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed that filtering has occurred or how many results are now displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2544: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches === 1 ? '' : 's'} found`); inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open/_close functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The site search combobox opens and closes the suggestion listbox by updating internal state and calling suggestionListRenderer.show/hide, but it does not toggle aria-expanded on the search input element. Screen readers cannot convey whether the suggestion list is visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2140: Add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after _searchState.open = true;
  Line 2154: Add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after _searchState.open = false;

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (e.g., monthly to annual), prices update dynamically across all plan cards, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2448: Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`); after _updateSavingsMessage(period);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Screen readers assume the menu state is unknown until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: After _trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick); add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips _onChipClick missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a user removes an active filter by clicking a chip button, the filter is removed and the product grid updates, but the action is not announced. Screen reader users are not informed that the filter has been removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2727: Add liveRegion.announce(`Filter removed: ${label}`); at the start of the _onChipClick function before the switch statement, where label is derived from the chip text;

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as valid, the function removes the error description association but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1134: Add removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); after the existing attribute removal logic (or add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false'););

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not programmatically associated with the navigation drawer panel using aria-controls. Screen readers cannot identify which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: After _drawer is assigned, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));

issuesFound.title: Inline validation never sets aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The validation system adds/removes describedby/error messages but never toggles `aria-invalid`, so screen readers cannot know a field is in error.
issuesFound.fix:
In `markInvalid` add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and in `markValid` remove the attribute (`field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`).  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing initial aria‑expanded value
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is created the trigger receives `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` but no `aria-expanded` (default “false”). Screen readers therefore assume the menu is always expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
In `register` after setting `aria-controls` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile menu updates visual state but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After `_open` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` and after `_close` announce `STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Search input does not update aria‑expanded when suggestion list opens
issuesFound.explanation: The search box shows a suggestion list (ARIA `listbox`) but never toggles `aria-expanded` on the input, leaving the accessibility state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_open` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_close` set it back to `false`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change does not announce the new state
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly/annual pricing updates UI but does not send a live‑region message, so AT users cannot hear the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the UI, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Annual billing selected');` or the appropriate message based on `period`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Search input missing initial aria‑expanded value
issuesFound.explanation: The input is given `aria-controls` and `aria-autocomplete` but no initial `aria-expanded="false"`, so screen readers cannot know that the list is hidden initially.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating the listbox, call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Several interactive UI actions (e.g., opening the mobile nav, toggling the pricing period, expanding FAQ items) emit custom events but lack dedicated live‑region announcements, violating the requirement that each visible state change be announced.
issuesFound.explanation: Users relying on screen readers receive no audible feedback for these state changes.
issuesFound.fix: For each such action, add a call to `liveRegion.announce(...)` with a concise description of the new state immediately after the DOM change.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing initial aria‑expanded value
issuesFound.explanation: The parser only sets `aria-expanded` to `true` if the item is marked open; otherwise the attribute is absent, leaving the default undefined.
issuesFound.fix:
After `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` for items that are not initially open.  

---

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid without this state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1137: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger element does not update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed when activating the toggle.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1405: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1425: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger element aria-expanded not initialised on load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button. Assistive technologies may announce the button as expanded by default or lack state context on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1465: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger elements aria-expanded not initialised on load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1665: if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger (item.trigger). The close() function correctly sets "false", creating an inconsistent state where the open action is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2435: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters updates count without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the product count badge textContent (_updateCountBadge) but does not announce this change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the number of visible products changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2025: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error description but does not remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute to "false". The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technologies even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1147: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they are given aria-haspopup and aria-controls attributes, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false". The initial collapsed state is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1795: After panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing returnFocus implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function accepts a returnFocus parameter but does not implement focus management when closing the dropdown. Keyboard users lose focus context when the dropdown closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1769: At the end of _closeInstance, add: if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus();

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded the panel is shown, but the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `true`. Users of assistive tech cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of `open`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off billing‑period switch changes visual state but never updates `aria-pressed`, which is required for a toggle button to convey its state to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the period, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));` or update the attribute in `setPeriod`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not set aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the off‑canvas menu is hidden the drawer element is only stripped of CSS classes; it never receives `aria-hidden="true"`, so the element remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close` add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` and in `open` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod updates prices without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates all plan card price displays dynamically but does not announce the billing period change or new prices via a live region. Users are not notified that the pricing context has switched (e.g. monthly to annual).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Screen readers cannot identify which fields have errors.
issuesFound.fix: In the else block (when !result.valid), add: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: pricing price changes not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.fix: After line 2500 (after updating all plan cards), add: `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1420: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened');` in _openInstance.
  Line 1432: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');` in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced to screen readers. Users may not know the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1318: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` in open function.
  Line 1338: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` in close function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Product filter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed when activated. The HTML contract specifies these buttons should have aria-pressed, but the JavaScript only toggles CSS classes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2724: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate adds error‑message elements and CSS error classes but never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the failing input, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult when result.valid is false, call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and remove it when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not set aria‑expanded on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer’s visibility but never updates aria‑expanded on the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false') when closing.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period switch does not announce changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates price numbers but provides no aria‑live announcement, so users of assistive technology may miss the price change.
issuesFound.fix: After setPeriod updates the UI, call liveRegion.announce(`Pricing switched to ${period}`); or update a dedicated status element with aria‑live="polite".

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are hidden with hidden attribute but lack aria‑hidden when shown/hidden via JavaScript
issuesFound.explanation: The panel’s hidden attribute is toggled, but aria‑hidden is not kept in sync, which can cause brief accessibility‑tree inconsistencies.
issuesFound.fix: When opening, remove aria‑hidden; when closing, set aria‑hidden="true".

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The total number of visible products is reflected in a badge element, but the element lacks an aria‑live region, so changes are not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Add role="status" and aria‑live="polite" to the badge (or update a hidden live‑region) whenever the count changes.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not set aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The “annual/monthly” switch ( [data‑billing‑toggle] ) changes visual state but never updates aria‑pressed, leaving screen‑reader users without feedback on the current selection.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)); or update the data‑state accordingly.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid form fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 980: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes aria-describedby but does not clear aria-invalid, leaving fields incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 991: Add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when registered. The register function sets aria-haspopup but not aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: After line 1480 (after setting aria-controls), add: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer. Keyboard users can tab outside the drawer to background elements.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The email field validation relies on inlineValidator which does not set aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot identify the invalid field.

issuesFound.title: contactForm validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Contact form validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields because it uses the broken inlineValidator.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Wizard step validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields because it uses the broken inlineValidator.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button that opens the mobile drawer never receives an aria‑controls attribute pointing to the drawer’s id, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Assign a unique id to the drawer (e.g., id="mobile-drawer") and set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'mobile-drawer') during mobileNav.init().

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer is closed, it is merely hidden via CSS (scroll‑lock) and does not receive aria‑hidden="true", so the element remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove the attribute in open().

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard step changes do not have a dedicated live‑region announcement for each step transition
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard emits a custom wizard:change event but does not write a message to an aria‑live region, so screen‑reader users receive no audible cue when the step changes.
issuesFound.fix: After _goToStep call liveRegion.announce(`Step ${_current+1} of ${_steps.length}: ${_steps[_current].title}`); or update a hidden status element with aria‑live="polite".

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup incorrect error props causing broken aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component is invoked with an `error` prop, but the component interface expects `errorId` and `errorMessage` props. Consequently, the ErrorMessage element is never rendered, and the TextInput's aria-describedby references a non-existent ID 'newsletter-email-error', leaving screen reader users unaware of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1912: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm ErrorMessage incorrect prop usage
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component receives its content as children rather than the required `message` prop. Since the component only renders when the `message` prop is provided, this server error message is never displayed visually or announced to assistive technology users, preventing them from knowing the form submission failed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2090: <ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" message="Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again." />

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm ErrorMessage incorrect prop usage
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component is rendered with content as children, but the component interface expects a `message` prop. The component returns null when `message` is undefined, so the error text "Something went wrong..." is never rendered or announced to screen reader users, hiding submission failure feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1942: <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." />

issuesFound.fix: Line 1025: `<TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" required aria-required="true" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” <input> missing aria‑invalid when invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component does not set aria‑invalid even when the invalid prop is true, so error state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1082: `<TextInput id="contact-name" type="text" name="name" required aria-required="true" aria-invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} ... />`

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” <input> missing aria‑invalid when invalid
issuesFound.fix: Line 1095: `<TextInput id="contact-email" type="email" name="email" required aria-required="true" aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} ... />`

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” <input> missing aria‑invalid when invalid
issuesFound.fix: Line 1108: `<TextInput id="contact-subject" type="text" name="subject" required aria-required="true" aria-invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} ... />`

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” <textarea> missing aria‑invalid when invalid
issuesFound.fix: Line 1122: `<Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-required="true" aria-invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} ... />`

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” <textarea> broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.fix: Add `<ErrorMessage id="contact-message-error" message={message.state.error} />` after the textarea or remove the reference.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active links missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links indicating the current page (item.current) use a visual class but lack aria-current="page", preventing screen reader users from identifying the active page in the navigation list.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing button functions as a toggle in a radio-like group but lacks aria-pressed to communicate whether it is the selected option.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button functions as a toggle in a radio-like group but lacks aria-pressed to communicate whether it is the selected option.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard primary CTA missing descriptive aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA link inside the pricing plan card has generic text (e.g., "Get started for free") without the plan name. In a grid of similar cards, this results in non-unique link names for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, so screen readers cannot announce the error state of form fields using this component.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~570: Add `aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined}` to the input element.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button uses aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~665: Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button props.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for visual marquee looping lacks aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce the integration partners twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~920: Add `aria-hidden="true"` to the second ul element (the clone for visual looping).

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing toggle buttons show the selected state visually but do not communicate it programmatically via aria-pressed, leaving screen reader users unaware of the current selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1285: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}` to the monthly button. Line ~1292: Add `aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}` to the annual button.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion-style section toggles in the mobile navigation have aria-controls but no aria-expanded attribute, preventing screen reader users from knowing which sections are open.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~755: Add `aria-expanded={isExpanded}` to the button element.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup using incorrect error prop
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects `errorMessage` prop but receives `error`, causing validation errors not to be displayed to users, preventing them from correcting form mistakes.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1600: Change `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}`.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links navigation is one of multiple nav elements on the page but lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from the main and footer navigations.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~440: Add `aria-label="Skip navigation"` to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, preventing assistive technology from announcing error states.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~585: Add `aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined}` to the textarea element.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only the "›" character and lacks an aria-label, making its function unknown to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1510: Add `aria-label="Next testimonial"` to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link applies a visual modifier class (nav-link--current) but lacks aria-current="page", so screen readers cannot identify the current page in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~725: Add `aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}` to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal incorrectly placed on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is incorrectly placed on the backdrop overlay instead of the dialog element itself, and the dialog lacks aria-modal, potentially causing assistive technology to interact with content outside the modal while it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1830: Remove `aria-modal="true"` from the overlay div. Line ~1835: Add `aria-modal="true"` to the div with `role="dialog"`.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ disclosure button has aria-controls pointing to the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the answer is currently visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1430: Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button props.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section lacks aria-labelledby or aria-label, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the purpose of this landmark region when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~805: Add `aria-labelledby="hero-heading"` to the section element.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non‑existent id
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for the email address includes aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but no element with that id exists, resulting in a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1418: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? undefined : undefined} />

(removed the broken aria-describedby attribute)
